hero.title: Ana Vallejo Rivera
hero.subtitle: Designer & Web Developer
hero.description: I build modern websites and digital experiences that are beautiful, functional, and help businesses grow.

projects.description: A collection of recent work showcasing web design and development projects for clients in various industries.

skills.description: Web design and frontend development skills that allow me to participate in different phases of a project, from structure and interface design to development.
skills.learning: Always learning and expanding my skills. Currently diving deeper into advanced React patterns, accessibility best practices, and modern CSS techniques to create better digital experiences

workProcess.description: A proven process for creating successful web projects from concept to launch.
workProcess.researchDesc: Understanding your goals, target audience, and project requirements
workProcess.wireframesDesc: Creating the structure and foundation of a website before design and development
workProcess.designDesc: Designing attractive and brand-consistent interfaces using Figma
workProcess.developmentDesc: Building responsive and functional websites with clean code
workProcess.launchDesc: Testing, optimizing, and deploying your website live

about.bio: I'm a self-taught web designer and developer with training in IT systems administration. My interest in web development started out of curiosity about how websites work and, over time, became a genuine motivation to learn to design and build digital products.
about.technologies: During this process I've been studying technologies like HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, and I continue to expand my knowledge in web development, especially in tools and environments related to WordPress and e-commerce. I'm currently taking an online course for a higher degree in Web Application Development (DAW), which allows me to continue strengthening my technical training while continuing to learn in a practical way.
about.interests: I'm interested in both the technical side of building a website and designing clear, functional, and easy-to-use interfaces. I especially enjoy the process of transforming an idea into a real and functional website. For me, a good website is not just about aesthetics, but about creating something useful, well-structured, and that offers a good user experience.
about.approach: By combining design and development, I try to bring a practical vision to projects. Understanding how an interface is designed and how it's technically built helps me create more coherent and realistic solutions.
about.learning: I'm currently continuing to learn and develop projects that allow me to improve my skills and gain real experience in web development.
about.usabilityDesc: Designing clear and easy-to-use interfaces, paying attention to content structure and ensuring simple navigation for users.
about.codeDesc: Writing clean and understandable code, following basic best practices to make projects easy to maintain and improve.
about.problemDesc: When a technical problem arises, I like to analyze it, research different solutions, and test until I find the one that works best in each case.
about.continuousDesc: I continue to expand my knowledge in web development and design, exploring new tools, technologies, and ways to improve my projects.

contact.description: Do you have a project in mind? I'd love to hear about it and discuss how I can help you bring your vision to life.
contact.openToWork: I'm currently available for freelance projects and open to positions as a junior designer/developer. Whether you need a complete website, a landing page, or custom WordPress development, I'd love to discuss your needs.

appExamenes.title: Exam Prep App - Study Platform for Students
appExamenes.description: An interactive quiz application for vocational students with multiple-choice questions, progress tracking, and 15 programming units.
appExamenes.overview: A modern and interactive web platform designed to help Development Applications students prepare for their exams. The application allows users to select from different subjects (English, Business Management and Planning, and Programming) and take tests with automatic progress tracking. This was my first project using React and Tailwind CSS, focused on practicing importing and using external libraries.
appExamenes.problem: As a vocational student, I needed a practical tool to prepare for my exams in an interactive way. A simple text document wasn't enough - I needed an application that could track my progress, show me my mistakes, and keep me motivated. Additionally, I wanted to create a real project to apply my React and Tailwind CSS knowledge.
problemQuestions: How can I practice exams in an organized way?
How to track my progress across different subjects?
How to get immediate feedback on my answers?
How can I build a real React project?
goals: Create an intuitive interface for selecting and taking tests
Implement a progress and results tracking system
Offer immediate feedback on correct and incorrect answers
Support multiple subjects with various thematic units
Practice integrating external libraries in React
Apply Tailwind CSS for modern, clean, and responsive design
appExamenes.design: The design was iterative and student-centered. I created a clear flow: home > select subject > select unit > take test > view results. I designed expandable cards for each subject with clear access buttons. The interface is clean and motivating, with visual progress indicators. I used subtle colors that don't cause eye strain during studying. Smooth transitions make navigation pleasant.
appExamenes.development: Built entirely with React using reusable components and custom hooks. Tailwind CSS provided utility styles and ensured responsive design across all devices. Implemented React Router for navigation between sections. Used useState and useEffect to manage the state of answers and result calculations. Questions are structured in JSON for scalability. Added localStorage for persistence of user progress between sessions.
appExamenes.result: The application successfully launched with 3 main subjects and 15 complete units in Programming. It's a functional tool that works perfectly on mobile, tablet, and desktop. The project demonstrated key concepts: React componentization, state management, responsive design with Tailwind, and good UX practices. I continue improving the application with new questions and features based on feedback. It's an important achievement as my first serious React project.

footer.designedBuilt: Designed and built by Ana Vallejo
